Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. A Decade of Slug 171 upvotes, 10 comments
  2. Unsloth Studio 39 upvotes, 2 comments
  3. Python 3.15's JIT is now back on track 77 upvotes, 7 comments
  4. FFmpeg 8.1 283 upvotes, 44 comments
  5. The Plumbing of Everyday Magic 23 upvotes, 1 comments
  6. Leanstral: Open-source agent for trustworthy coding and formal proof engineering 723 upvotes, 177 comments
  7. Kagi Small Web 631 upvotes, 177 comments
  8. Finding a CPU Design Bug in the Xbox 360 (2018) 137 upvotes, 41 comments
  9. It Took Me 30 Years to Solve This VFX Problem – Green Screen Problem [video] 56 upvotes, 15 comments
  10. Microsoft's 'unhackable' Xbox One has been hacked by 'Bliss' 386 upvotes, 160 comments
  11. Meta, TikTok let harmful content rise after evidence outrage drove engagement 31 upvotes, 7 comments
  12. Show HN: Antfly: Distributed, Multimodal Search and Memory and Graphs in Go 65 upvotes, 21 comments
  13. Edge.js: Run Node apps inside a WebAssembly sandbox 35 upvotes, 9 comments
  14. 'The Secret Agent': Exploring a Vibrant, yet Violent Brazil (2025) 81 upvotes, 33 comments
  15. Reverse-engineering Viktor and making it open source 131 upvotes, 59 comments
  16. Show HN: Crust – A CLI framework for TypeScript and Bun 44 upvotes, 17 comments
  17. Font Smuggler – Copy hidden brand fonts into Google Docs 137 upvotes, 69 comments
  18. GPT‑5.4 Mini and Nano 158 upvotes, 97 comments
  19. Ryugu asteroid samples contain all DNA and RNA building blocks 130 upvotes, 81 comments
  20. OpenSUSE Kalpa 100 upvotes, 65 comments
  21. Show HN: March Madness Bracket Challenge for AI Agents Only 53 upvotes, 31 comments
  22. Java 26 is here, and with it a solid foundation for the future 80 upvotes, 54 comments
  23. Torturing Rustc by Emulating HKTs 4 upvotes, 0 comments
  24. 'It's sweet. It's bitter. It's ours.' The chocolate ritual that binds my family 4 upvotes, 0 comments
  25. Node.js needs a virtual file system 164 upvotes, 143 comments
  26. Toward automated verification of unreviewed AI-generated code 57 upvotes, 46 comments
  27. Honda is killing its EVs 97 upvotes, 88 comments
  28. Get Shit Done: A Meta-Prompting, Context Engineering and Spec-Driven Dev System 14 upvotes, 9 comments
  29. Illinois Introducing Operating System Account Age Bill 182 upvotes, 226 comments
  30. Spice Data (YC S19) Is Hiring a Product Specialist 0 upvotes, 0 comments
